title: Breath Awareness
blurb: The simplest anchor: resting your attention on the breath.
lead: Breath awareness is the foundation of most meditation. You simply notice the natural breath — and gently return whenever the mind wanders.

blocks.text: Breath awareness means resting your attention on the sensations of breathing — the air at your nostrils, the rise and fall of your chest or belly.
You are not trying to control the breath or stop thoughts. Each time you notice your mind has wandered and return to the breath, you strengthen attention and calm. The returning is the practice.

items: Sit comfortably, upright but relaxed. Close your eyes or soften your gaze.
Notice where you feel the breath most clearly — nose, chest, or belly.
Rest your attention there, following each in-breath and out-breath.
When you notice thinking (you will, often), gently return to the breath — no frustration needed.
Continue for a few minutes to start; build up over time.

items.label: Tradition
items.value: Common to nearly all contemplative paths

blocks.text: Believing you're 'failing' when the mind wanders. Wandering is normal — noticing and returning is exactly the skill you're building.

resources.title: Healthy Minds Program
resources.whyHere: Free, structured beginner training in awareness.
